Divide 58'-16" by 6. You get the Sun's motion for 4 hours.
This comes to 0 -9'-43". From this you can work out the
motion for 25 minutes after finding it out for one hour. So for
the interval of 4 h. 25 m. the Sun's motion is !0 m. 43 s. By
adding this to the Sun's it comes to 4S-21 '-58'-47*. In the same
manner you can calculate the longitudes of the other planets as
well In the case of fast-moving planets however there is" a

small correction noticed on page 61. I shall give you now the
consolidated results in the following Table : —
Lagna -6M(r-32'-4*. Venus .. 5'.290-33'-4'
Sun =4-21-58-47 Saturn -7-15-0-22,
Moon =10-10-51-45. Rahu =6-18-52-5.
Mars =4-26-26-0. Ketu =0-18-52-5.
Ret. Mercury —4-25-17-0. Guiika =9-0-8-31.
Jupiter -5-13-0-12. Mandi =9-23-23-52.
Note Here Mercury is retrograde. Hence in his case
and in that of Rahu the motion in degrees etc. is to be deducted
from the position given for 5-30 A.M.
